reasoning: A massive US/Israeli attack on Iranian nuclear facilities ('Operation Midnight Hammer') in June 2025 provoked parliamentary votes and threats to close the Strait of Hormuz, along with reports in July of mine-laying preparations. However, the Supreme National Security Council never followed through to enact closing the Strait. Since June, 'commercial traffic continues to flow uninterrupted.' While Iran seized a Western-managed tanker (M/V Talara) on November 14, 2025, they released it just five days later on November 19, signaling a clear unwillingness to escalate to a severe restriction or halt of maritime traffic. With only 38 days left in 2025 and traffic flowing despite historically unprecedented strikes on Iran's nuclear infrastructure earlier this year, it is exceedingly unlikely that Iran will shift its strategy and decisively close the strait before year's end. The market was at 4% in mid-November, and the release of the tanker since then justifies pushing the estimate down to 3%.
